_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Chronic pyelonephritis, renal arterial disease, calculus disease
Benign Diseases: Multicystic or dysplastic kidneys, reflux nephropathy, obstructive uropathy, xanthogranulomatous pyelonephritis, calculus disease, and protein-losing nephropathy
Carcinoma: localized and metastatic renal cell carcinoma
In paediatric conditions: non-functioning renal segment of a duplex system associated with ectopic ureteroceles, VUR, or obstruction
Benign/malignant causes: Upper urinary tract tumors also for large, high-grade tumors of the renal pelvis and proximal ureter that are organ-confined or locally advanced
Tuberculous non-functional kidney: calcification of the renal parenchyma and/or multiple infundibular stenosis or ureteric strictures

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
CT/CT-IVP/MRI/MR Urogram report confirming the need for surgery

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the CT/CT-IVP/MRI/MR Urogram report confirming the need for surgery submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or CT/CT-IVP/MRI/MR Urogram reports are not indicative of surgery.
Missing or incomplete clinical notes, evaluation findings, or planned line of treatment at pre-authorization.
CT/CT-IVP/MRI/MR Urogram report not submitted or does not confirm the need for surgery.
Missing intra-operative photograph or histopathology report at claim submission.
Detailed indoor case papers, procedure/operative notes, or discharge summary not submitted or incomplete at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Nephroureterectomy with cuff of bladder is the removal of the kidney, ureter, and bladder cuff and lymph nodes.

For patients with high-risk upper tract urothelial carcinoma (UTUC), renal pelvis or ureteral tumour, radical nephroureterectomy with ipsilateral bladder cuff excision (RNU + BCE) is the standard treatment.
The da Vinci robot system has been widely used in laparoscopy urologic surgery.
Nephroureterectomy is a minimally invasive procedure to remove the entire kidney along with the ureter.
It is also recommended for multifocal, recurrent, low-grade tumors.
The procedure involves removal of the kidney, ureter, and bladder cuff via a thoracoabdominal or flank approach.
Both Open and Laparoscopic Nephroureterectomy are common procedures.
Nephroureterectomy with resection of the ipsilateral bladder cuff is the gold-standard treatment for tumors.
surgical_options: Nephroureterectomy involves three operative steps: (I) nephrectomy, (II) ureteral extirpation, and (III) resection of the intravesical and intramural ureteral segment, known as Bladder Cuff Excision (BCE).
Bladder Cuff Excision (BCE) techniques include: ONU with formal BCE, Endoscopic BCE, Intussusception technique for BCE, LNU for BCE, LNU with transvesical technique for BCE.

Open Partial/hemi Nephrectomy (ONU): Excision of the kidney and upper ureter by retroperitoneoscopic approach and bladder cuff excision by lower abdominal incision-open surgery (not minimally invasive).

Laparoscopic Partial/hemi Nephrectomy (LNU): Excision of the kidney and upper ureter by retroperitoneoscopic approach and bladder cuff excision by lower abdominal incision-open surgery (not minimally invasive).
Surgical management of nephrectomy, using either open or laparoscopic approach, for non-functional kidney.
Radical nephroureterectomy (RNU), either open or laparoscopic, with excision of a cuff of bladder mucosa, is the gold standard for Upper urinary tract tumors.
Laparoscopic approach, open excision of the distal ureter and cuff, endoscopic ureteral "pluck" technique, and endoscopic ureteral “intussusception” technique.
